title: Indus Valley Civilization
excerpt: One of the world's earliest urban cultures, flourishing along the Indus River around 2500 BCE.
content:
The Indus Valley Civilization (also called the Harappan Civilization) developed sophisticated cities such as Harappa and Mohenjo-daro, with planned streets, drainage systems, and standardized weights.

Archaeological evidence suggests extensive trade networks and a script that remains undeciphered. The civilization declined around 1900 BCE, possibly due to climate change and shifts in river courses.

title: The Mauryan Empire
excerpt: India's first large empire, unifying much of the subcontinent under Chandragupta and Ashoka.
content:
Founded by Chandragupta Maurya with guidance from Chanakya, the Mauryan Empire (c. 322–185 BCE) created a centralized administration and vast road networks.

Emperor Ashoka later embraced Buddhism and promoted dharma through edicts carved on pillars and rocks across the realm. The empire's decline followed Ashoka's death and succession disputes.
